input.background: The Scandinavian simvastatin survival study (4S) was a randomized clinical trial designed to evaluate the effects of the cholesterol-lowering agent simvastatin in patients with coronary heart disease. Over 5.4 years of follow-up, the treatment group consisting of 2221 individuals experienced 111 fatal heart attacks. The placebo group of 2223 individuals experienced 189 such events.
input.question: Test the difference for significance.

output.answer: A. Hypotheses. $H_0: p_1=p_2$ versus $H_{\mathrm{a}}: p_1 \neq p_2$ B. Test statistic. $z_{\text {stat }}=4.66$ C. $P$-value. $P$ (two-tailed) $=0.0000032$ (highly significant) D. Significance level (optional). The evidence against the null hypothesis is significant at $\alpha=0.01$.
